61: **Norway**, level with one other team at the top of **Group I** on 6 points, meet **France**, equally level on 6 points with positions not yet separated — both sides already assured of progression and separated only by a single goal in their respective goal differences. First place in **Group I** is the only prize left to play for, but that is prize enough: the bracket advantage it carries could shape each side's path all the way to the later knockouts.
62: **Senegal**, level with one other team at the bottom of **Group I** on 0 points, face **Iraq**, also level on 0 points with positions not yet separated — and both sides knowing only a win gives them any mathematical lifeline, should extraordinary results elsewhere intervene. This is an elimination contest in all but name, and the desperation of two winless teams clashing on the final group day should produce an open, high-stakes encounter from the first whistle.
65: **Cabo Verde**, level with one other team in **Group H** on 2 points and positions not yet separated, take on fourth-placed **Saudi Arabia**, who sit bottom of **Group H** on 1 point. **Cabo Verde** need a result to stay in the hunt for the second knockout spot, while **Saudi Arabia** must win and rely on other results — making this a match where the pressure falls squarely on both sets of players despite the gap in the standings.
66: **Uruguay**, level with one other team in **Group H** on 2 points and positions not yet separated, face group leaders **Spain**, who sit top of **Group H** on 4 points. **Spain** will be pushing to confirm first place outright, while **Uruguay** know a positive result here — combined with the right outcome in the other **Group H** game — could be the difference between the knockouts and an early flight home.
63: **Egypt**, top of **Group G** on 4 points, face **IR Iran**, who are level with one other team in the group on 2 points with positions not yet separated. **Egypt** can seal first place with a win, but **Iran** arrive knowing that anything less than a positive result may cede the second knockout spot to a rival — expect a cagey, tactically loaded affair with both teams acutely aware of what is unfolding in the other **Group G** fixture.
64: **New Zealand**, fourth in **Group G** on 1 point, face **Belgium**, who are level with one other team in the group on 2 points with positions not yet separated. **Belgium** need a win to take control of their own destiny in the race for the knockouts, while **New Zealand** — with nothing to lose — will look to play spoiler and make it as uncomfortable as possible for a side that has yet to find their best form this stage.
55: **Curaçao**, level with one other team in **Group E** on 1 point and positions not yet separated, face **Côte d'Ivoire**, who sit 2nd in **Group E** on 3 points. **Côte d'Ivoire** need at least a point to stay in the frame for progression, but **Curaçao** — knowing only a win gives them any hope — will push forward, making this a lively, open contest with real stakes at both ends.
56: **Ecuador**, level with one other team in **Group E** on 1 point and positions not yet separated, meet group leaders **Germany**, who sit top of **Group E** on 6 points with a commanding +7 goal difference. **Germany** arrive already through and free to rotate, yet protecting first place remains worthwhile — for **Ecuador**, only a win combined with the right result in the parallel fixture keeps their knockout hopes alive.
57: **Japan**, level with one other team in **Group F** on 4 points and positions not yet separated, host 3rd-placed **Sweden**, who sit on 3 points in the group. **Sweden** must win to stay in contention and have little choice but to push forward, while **Japan** know a draw may well be enough to confirm their place in the knockouts — a tense, chess-like battle that could hinge on a single moment.
58: **Tunisia**, rooted to the bottom of **Group F** on 0 points after two defeats, face **Netherlands**, who are level with one other team at the top of the group on 4 points and positions not yet separated. **Netherlands** will be eager to seal first place and secure the better knockout route, while **Tunisia** — with nothing left to lose — could prove dangerous despite the gulf between the two sides in the standings.
59: **Türkiye**, bottom of **Group D** on 0 points after two defeats, face group leaders **USA**, who sit top of **Group D** on a perfect 6 points. **USA** are already through and may rotate with the knockouts in mind, but protecting their goal difference remains a priority — **Türkiye** arrive desperate to salvage something, and only a win keeps the faintest mathematical hope alive.
60: **Paraguay**, level with one other team in **Group D** on 3 points and positions not yet separated, face **Australia**, equally level on 3 points in the group with positions not yet separated. One wins and advances, one exits — both sides know this is as close to a knockout fixture as the group stage gets, and expect an intense, physical contest where neither team can afford to blink.
